title: Compat API endpoint for "Inspect Container" response "Image" field should contain ImageID.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

The compat API endpoint to inspect container `/v1.40/containers/{container_name}/json` response has a filed named "Image", on Docker it contains the image hash, but on Podman, it contains the image name. This causes one or more operations in Docker-PY module to fail.

_Note_, the compat container _list_ endpoint `/v1.40/containers/json` _does_ include the image ID in field `ImageID`, so its omission in the single container inspect endpoint is a bug.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pull alpine:3.16`

2. `podman run --name my_container -i -t alpine:3.16 /bin/true`  (don't include `--rm`)

4. `podman container inspect my_container` 

5. `curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock -H accept:application/json http://localhost/v1.40/containers/my_container/json`

**Describe the results you received:**
Image field in the response shows image name:
```
{
   "Id":"02e2d9d92829389c0a3d012effb932cd09687f9a2995c52c5ff3a7d3817d9ae7",
   "Created":"2022-09-16T08:51:15.331628601Z",
   "Path":"/bin/true",
   "Args":[
      "/bin/true"
   ],
   "State":{
      "Status":"exited",
      "Running":false,
      "Paused":false,
      "Restarting":false,
      "OOMKilled":false,
      "Dead":false,
      "Pid":0,
      "ExitCode":0,
      "Error":"",
      "StartedAt":"2022-09-16T08:51:15.421336931Z",
      "FinishedAt":"2022-09-16T08:51:15.419473831Z",
      "Health":{
         "Status":"",
         "FailingStreak":0,
         "Log":null
      }
   },
   "Image":"docker.io/library/alpine:3.16",
   "ResolvConfPath":"/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/02e2d9d92829389c0a3d012effb932cd09687f9a2995c52c5ff3a7d3817d9ae7/userdata/resolv.conf",
   "HostnamePath":"/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/02e2d9d92829389c0a3d012effb932cd09687f9a2995c52c5ff3a7d3817d9ae7/userdata/hostname",
   "HostsPath":"/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/02e2d9d92829389c0a3d012effb932cd09687f9a2995c52c5ff3a7d3817d9ae7/userdata/hosts",
   "LogPath":"",
   "Name":"/my_container",
   "RestartCount":0,
   "Driver":"overlay",
   "Platform":"linux",
   "MountLabel":"",
   "ProcessLabel":"",
   "AppArmorProfile":"",
   "ExecIDs":[
      
   ],
   "HostConfig":{
     ...
   }
}
```



**Describe the results you expected:**
Image field in the response should be "sha256:xyzhashxyz"
```
{
   "Id":"02e2d9d92829389c0a3d012effb932cd09687f9a2995c52c5ff3a7d3817d9ae7",
   "Created":"2022-09-16T08:51:15.331628601Z",
   "Path":"/bin/true",
   "Args":[
      "/bin/true"
   ],
   "State":{
      "Status":"exited",
      "Running":false,
      "Paused":false,
      "Restarting":false,
      "OOMKilled":false,
      "Dead":false,
      "Pid":0,
      "ExitCode":0,
      "Error":"",
      "StartedAt":"2022-09-16T08:51:15.421336931Z",
      "FinishedAt":"2022-09-16T08:51:15.419473831Z",
      "Health":{
         "Status":"",
         "FailingStreak":0,
         "Log":null
      }
   },
   "Image":"9c6f0724472873bb50a2ae67a9e7adcb57673a183cea8b06eb778dca859181b5",
   "ResolvConfPath":"/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/02e2d9d92829389c0a3d012effb932cd09687f9a2995c52c5ff3a7d3817d9ae7/userdata/resolv.conf",
   "HostnamePath":"/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/02e2d9d92829389c0a3d012effb932cd09687f9a2995c52c5ff3a7d3817d9ae7/userdata/hostname",
   "HostsPath":"/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/02e2d9d92829389c0a3d012effb932cd09687f9a2995c52c5ff3a7d3817d9ae7/userdata/hosts",
   "LogPath":"",
   "Name":"/my_container",
   "RestartCount":0,
   "Driver":"overlay",
   "Platform":"linux",
   "MountLabel":"",
   "ProcessLabel":"",
   "AppArmorProfile":"",
   "ExecIDs":[
      
   ],
   "HostConfig":{
       ...
   }
}
```

_Note_, the compat container _list_ endpoint `/v1.40/containers/json` _does_ include the image ID in filed `ImageID`, so it is not affected by this bug.

This is the section of code in Docker-Py library that crashes:
https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/923e067dddc3d4b86e4e620a99fcdcdafbd17a98/docker/models/containers.py#L37-L40
Notice it is expecting an ImageID in the form `sha256:xyzhashxyz`, but it does not find it, so it falls back to field `Image` then splits the value `alpine:3.16` on the colon, returning just "3.16" instead of the image hash, this  causes the image lookup to fail.

Minimal reproduction in Python:
```python
import docker
client = docker.from_env()
container = client.containers.get("my_container")
image = container.image    # <-- crashes here
```
error:
```python
docker.errors.ImageNotFound: 404 Client Error for http+docker://localhost/v1.40/images/3.16/json: Not Found ("failed to find image 3.16: docker.io/library/3.16: No such image")
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.1
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

  kernel: 5.18.11-xanmod1-MANJARO

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:

    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true

  slirp4netns:

      slirp4netns version 1.2.0
      commit: 656041d45cfca7a4176f6b7eed9e4fe6c11e8383

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 4
      libseccomp: 2.5.4
  swapFree: 11046121472
  swapTotal: 17179865088
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Manjaro official podman package, v4.1.1
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

No changes in behaviour between my version and latest codebase.

comments.body:
Thank you for reaching out, @ashleysommer!

I can reproduce using the current HEAD.  @boaz0, interested in tackling the bug?

@vrothberg 
I've just realized I made a mistake. The API response type for Container Inspect for the Docker API v1.40 does not define an "ImageID" field.
See the definition:
https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/api/types/types.go#L449-L481


After going through the relevant Podman code and Docker code, I see where the misunderstanding lies.

This is where Docker populates the `Image` field, it uses the image hash:
https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/a263241ea16f1b018fc1c8f5c6564d84f1153543/daemon/inspect.go#L170

Podman does the same, correctly:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/4ba6ee306088a4d322fe28f292bf5d5c4d17b778/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers.go#L491

So I'm not sure where this bug is being manifested. Somewhere the "Image" field is being overwritten with  `imageName`. I'm not familiar enough Go lang to investigate any further.

I've updated the original post to reflect my findings.
I think this might be fixed already in main.

Hmm, you're right.
https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/6411f57e789ecef322c120d34ad634495e54d257

I checked for recent commits in that file because I thought it seems like something that might've been changed (behaviour in code is different to behaviour I saw in the release), but when I checked yesterday I didn't see anything recent around that area, and I even checked git blame on that line of code, but didn't see anything. But today I checked again and its pretty obvious there was a commit last month in master that fixed it.

Fixed by https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/6411f57e789ecef322c120d34ad634495e54d257
